hadiths.chapter: 16. WHAT DEFILES CLOTHING AND THE BODY
hadiths.ar:
162  -  وَّق َّال مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلن  ُّعْمَّان ِ لِأ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  إ َّج ْزُّح ُّ مِن َّ إَّلْح َّلاَّءِ ق َّا َّسْي َّن ْح ِى  ن ِالْمَّاءِ ق َّي َّق َّغُّ ي َّوْن ِى  ق ِى   
ذ َّلِك َّ إَّلْمَّاءِ إَّل َّذ ِى  إِسْي َّن ْح َّي ْث ُّ ن ِهِ ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} was asked about a woman who has 
only one shirt and a child who urinates on her. What should She do?  
Imam {a.s} said: 'She should wash the shirt once a day.'. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 141 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 16, H. 161

hadiths.chapter: 16. WHAT DEFILES CLOTHING AND THE BODY
hadiths.ar:
163  -  وَّق َّال إ َّن ُّو إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ مُّوسَّى ن ْن ُّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ  عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  طِي ن ِ إَّلْمَّطَّزِ 
إ َّي  َّامٍ إ ِلأ َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّعْلَّمَّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّذْ ن َّح  َّسَّهُّ  س َّى ْء  ن َّعْذَّ إَّلْمَّطَّزِ ق َّا ِن ْ إ َّصَّان َّهُّ ن َّعْذَّ ي َّلاَّي َّه ِ إ َّي  َّامٍ ع َّسَّلَّهُّ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ طَّزِي ق اً ن َّط ِي ق اً لَّمْ ي َّع ْسِلْهُّ .
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn al-Nu'man asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s}:  
'I leave the restroom and perform istinja (cleansing) with water, and my clothing falls 
into the water used for istinja. Is there anything upon me?'  
Imam {a.s} said: 'There is no harm in it, and it does not affect you.'".  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 16, H. 162

hadiths.chapter: 16. WHAT DEFILES CLOTHING AND THE BODY
hadiths.ar:
164   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ إ َّن ُّو إَّلْأ َّعَّز ِّ إَّلن  َّح  َّاسُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّق َّالَّ: إ ِن ِّى  إ ُّعَّالِح ُّ إَّلذ َّوَّإب  َّ ق زُّن  َّمَّا ج َّزَّج ْث ُّ ن ِالل َّي ْلِ وَّ ق َّذْ 
ن َّالَّث ْ وَّ رَّإي َّث ْ ق َّي َّص ْزِب ُّ إ ِجْذَّإهَّا ن ِي َّذِهَّا إ َّوْ ن ِزِج ْلِهَّا ق َّي ُّن ْص َّحُّ عَّلَّى ي َّوْن ِى  ق َّق َّال .  
ولأ ن ا س ن ح زء إلذج اج ه  وإلحمامه  ي صي ث  إلي وب ، ولأ ن ا س ن ح زء ما طار ون وله، ولأ ن ا س ن ن ول كل س ى  إ كل لحمه ق ي صي ث  
إلي وب ، ولأ ن ا س ن لن ن  إلمزإ ة  إلمزص عه  ي صي ث  ق مي صها ق ي كي ز وي ي ن س .
hadiths.en:
Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim {a.s} said regarding rain -soaked 
mud: 'There is no harm if it comes into contact with clothing for three days, unless it is 
known that it has become contaminated with something impure after the rain. If it 
comes into contact after three days, it should be washed. However, if it is a clean path, 
there is no need to wash it.'. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al -Kafi, Volume 3, p. 13 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 1, p. 
267 • Al-Saraer, Part 3, p. 613 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 46 • Wasa’il Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Shi’a, 
vol. 3, p. 522 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 125 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 16, H. 163

hadiths.chapter: 16. WHAT DEFILES CLOTHING AND THE BODY
hadiths.ar:
165 - وَّ سُّن ِلَّ إَّلزِّص َّا عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّطَّا ُّ ق ِى  إَّلْحَّم َّامِ وَّ ق ِى  رِج ْلَّي ْهِ إَّلس  ُّق َّاق ُّ ق َّي َّطَّا ُّ إَّلْن َّوْلَّ وَّ إَّلن  ُّورَّة َّ ق َّي َّذْج ُّلُّ  
إَّلس  ُّق َّاق َّ إ َّي َّز  إ َّسْوَّذُّ مِم َّا وَّطِن َّهُّ مِن َّ إَّلْق َّذ َّرِ وَّ ق َّذْ ع َّسَّلَّهُّ كَّي ْف َّ ي َّصْن َّغُّ ن ِهِ وَّ ن ِزِج ْلِهِ إَّل َّي ِى  وَّطِ ى َّ ن ِهَّا إ َّ ي ُّ ح ْز ِي هِ إَّلْع َّسْلُّ إ َّمْ ي ُّح َّلِّلُّ 
[هِ مِن َّ إَّلزِّي حِ وَّ إَّلس  ُّق َّاق ِ 
ن َّعْذَّ ع َّسْلِهِ .  
ولأ ن ا س إ ن  ي ي ذلك  إلزج ل ق ى  إلحمام ن السوي ق  وإلذق ي ق  وإلن ح اله ، ق لي س ق ي ما ي ن ق غ إلن ذن  إ سزإف  إ ن ما إلأسزإف  ق ي ما إ ي لف  
إلماء وإ ص ز ن الن ذن .  وإلذم إ ذ إ إ صاب  إلي وب  ق لا ن ا س ن الصلاة  ق ي ه ما لم ي كن  مق ذإرة مق ذإر ذرهم وإف  ، وإلوإق ى  ما ي كون  
ور ن ه ذرهما وي لي ا، وما كان  ذون  إلذرهم إلوإق ى  ق ق ذ ي ح ث  ع سله   ولأ ن ا س ن الصلاة  ق ي ه.  وإ ن  كان  إلذم ذون  جمصه  ق لا ن ا س 
ن ا ن  لأ ي ع سل إ لأ إ ن  ي كون  ذم إلحي ض  ق ا ن ه ي ح ث  ع سل إلي وب  من ه ومن  إلن ول وإلمن ى  ق لي لا كان  إ و كي ي زإ وي عاذ من ه إلصلاة   
علم ن ه إ و لم ي علم.
hadiths.en:
Abu Al-Agharr al-Nakhkhas asked Imam Al-Sadiq {a.s}, saying:  
‘I tend to the riding animals, and sometimes I go out at night and they have urinated or 
defecated, so one of them strikes with its leg or hand [1causing it to splash on my 
clothing.'  
Imam {a.s} said: 'There is no harm in it.' 
[AL SADUQ] 
There is also no harm if chicken or pigeon droppings come into contact with clothing. 
Likewise, there is no issue with the droppings or urine of flying creatures or the urine 
of any animal whose meat is lawful to eat coming into contact with clothing. There is 
also no issue if the milk of a nursing woman touches her shirt, even in large amounts, 
once it dries. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 16, H. 164

hadiths.chapter: 16. WHAT DEFILES CLOTHING AND THE BODY
hadiths.en:
Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha {a.s} as asked about a man who steps in a public 
bath with cracked feet and steps on urine and lime, causing a black residue to enter 
the cracks. If he washes it, how should he treat it, and what about his foot that stepped 
on it? Is washing sufficient, or shou ld he also scrape under his nails? And he washes 
himself, only to then find smell emanating from his nails even when there is nothing?  
Imam {a.s} said: ‘There is nothing on him from the smell or the residue in the cracks after 
washing’  
[AL SADUQ] 
It is permissible for a person to rub themselves in the bath with flour, bran, or other 
beneficial substances, as there is no excessiveness in what benefits the body; 
extravagance applies to what wastes water or harms the body.  
Regarding blood that stains clothing: there is no issue with praying in it if the amount 
is not greater than a dirham in size. The "dirham-sized" measure weighs approximately 
one and one-third dirhams. If the blood is less than a full dirham, washing is preferred 
but not obligatory for prayer. However, if the blood is less than the size of a chickpea, 
there is no obligation to wash it unless it is menstrual blood. Menstrual blood, urine, 
and semen, regardless of quantity, must be washed from the clothing, a nd prayers 
must be repeated if performed without realizing its presence.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 16, H. 165

hadiths.chapter: 16. WHAT DEFILES CLOTHING AND THE BODY
hadiths.ar:
167  -  وَّ ق َّذْ رُّوِى  ق َّلاَّ س َّى ْءَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ 
ق َّا ِن ْ كَّان َّ لَّمْ ي َّن ْط ُّزْ وَّ لَّمْ ي َّطْلُّث ْ ق َّعَّلَّي ْهِ إ َّن ْ ي َّع ْسِلَّهُّ وَّ ي ُّعِي ذَّ صَّلاَّي َّهُّ .    
ولأ ن ا س ن ذم إلسمك  ق ى  إلي وب  إ ن  ي صلى  ق ي ه إلأن سان  ق لي لا  كان  إ و كي ي زإ.  ومن  إ صاب  ق لن سوي ه إ و عمامي ه إ و ي كي ه إ و ج ورن ه  
إ و ج ق ه من ى  إ و ن ول إ و ذم إ و ع ان ظ ق لا ن ا س ن الصلاة  ق ي ه وذ لك  لأن  إلصلاة  لأ ي ي م ق ى  س ى  من  هذ إ وجذة.  ومن  وق غ ي ون ه  
على جمار مي ث  ق لي س علي ه ع سله   ولأ ن ا س ن الصلاة  ق ي ه.  ولأ ن ا س إ ن  ي مس إلزج ل عط م إلمي ث  إ ذ إ ج ار  سن ه ، ولأ ن ا س إ ن  
ي ح عل سن  إلمي ث  للحى  مكان  سن ه . ومن  إ صاب  ي ون ه كلث  ج اف  ولم ي كن  ن كلث  صي ذ ق علي ه إ ن  ي زس ه ن الماء، وإ ن  كان  رطن ا  
ق علي ه إ ن  ي ع سله، وإ ن  كان  كلث  صي ذ وكان  ج اق ا ق لي س علي ه س ى  ، وإ ن  كان  رطن ا ق علي ه إ ن  ي زس ه ن الماء . ولأ ن ا س ن الصلاة  
ق ى  ي وب  إ صان ه ج مز لأن  إلله عز  وج ل   جزم س زن ها ولم ي حزم إلصلاة  ق ى  ي وب  إ صان ي ه. ق ا ما ق ى  ن ي ث  ق ي ه ج مز   ق لا ي ح ور  
إلصلاة  ق ي ه . ومن  ن ال ق ا صاب  ق ح ذ ة ن كي ه  من  ن وله ق صلى ي م ذ كز إ ن ه لم ي ع سله   ق علي ه إ ن  ي ع سله وي عي ذ صلاي ه . وإ ن  وق عث  
ق ا رة  ق ى  إلماء ي م ج زج ث  ق مس ث  على إلي ي اب  ق اع سل ما رإ ي ث  من  إ ي زها وما لم ي زة إن ص حه ن الماء.    وإ ن  كان  ن الزج ل ج زح 
سان ل ق ا صاب  ي ون ه من  ذمه ق لا ن ا س ن ا ن  لأ ي ع سل جي ى ي ن زإ  إ و ي ن ق طغ إلذم .
hadiths.en:
Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib {a.s} said: 'I do not concern myself with whether urine or 
water has touched me if I am unaware of it.'. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 253 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 
180 • Awaali al-La’aali, Part 1, p. 321 • Awaali al -La’aali, Part 3, p. 57 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 153 • Wasa’il Al -
Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 467 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 16, H. 166

hadiths.chapter: 16. WHAT DEFILES CLOTHING AND THE BODY
hadiths.en:
It is narrated regarding semen that if a man is junub (ritually impure due to 
seminal discharge) and gets up, looks, and finds nothing, then there is nothing upon 
him. However, if he does not look and does not check, he must wash himself and 
repeat his prayer.  
[AL SADUQ] 
There is no issue with the blood of fish on clothing, whether a small or large amount, 
when praying. If semen, urine, blood, or feces touch a man's cap, turban, waistband, 
socks, or shoes, there is no harm in praying in them, because prayer is not complete 
when performed solely in any of these items. 
If one's clothing touches a dead donkey, there is no need to wash it, and there is no 
issue with praying in it. 
There is no harm in touching the bone of a deceased person after a year has passed, 
and there is no issue with using a deceased person’s tooth as a replacement for one’s 
own. 
If a dry dog touches one’s clothing and it is not a hunting dog, it suffices to sprinkle 
water on it. If it is wet, it must be washed. For a hunting dog, if it is dry, there is nothing 
upon the person ; if it is wet, it suffices to sprinkle water. There is no issue with praying 
in a garment that has come into contact with alcohol because Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, 
has prohibited its consumption but has not prohibited praying in a garment affected 
by it. However, praying in a place where alcohol is present is  not permissible. 
If a person urinates and a spot of urine touches their thigh, then prays and later 
remembers that they did not wash it, they must wash it and repeat the prayer.  
If a mouse falls into water, exits, and then walks on clothing, one should wash the area 
where traces of it are seen and sprinkle water on the rest of the area.  
If a man has a bleeding wound that soils his clothing, there is no need to wash it until 
the wound heals or the bleeding stops. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 478 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 16, H. 167

hadiths.chapter: 16. WHAT DEFILES CLOTHING AND THE BODY
hadiths.en:
Abu Al-Hasan Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim {a.s} was asked about a eunuch 
who experiences difficulty with urination and notices wetness repeatedly.  
Imam {a.s} replied: 'He should perform ablution and sprinkle his clothing with water 
once during the day.'. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾ 
« .

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 16, H. 168

hadiths.chapter: 16. WHAT DEFILES CLOTHING AND THE BODY
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al-Kadhim {a.s} about a man 
whose clothing comes into contact with a dead dog.  
Imam {a.s} replied: 'He should sprinkle it with water and may pray in it, and there is no 
issue.'. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 277 • Al-Istibsar, Part 1, p. 
192 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 209 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 442 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 77, p. 55

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 16, H. 169

hadiths.chapter: 17. THE REASON WHY GHUSL (RITUAL BATH) IS
hadiths.en:
A group of Jews came to the Messenger of Allah {saws}, and the most learned 
among them asked him several questions.  
Amongst them, one (Jew) asked: 'Why did Allah {SWT} command ghusl (ritual bathing) 
after janabah (ritual impurity due to sexual discharge) but did not command ghusl after 
defecation and urination?' 
The Messenger of Allah {saws} replied:  
'When Adam ate from the tree, it coursed through his veins, hair, and skin.  
So when a man engages in intercourse, the fluid (semen) is released from every vein 
and hair in his body.  
Therefore, Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, has commanded his descendants to perform ghusl 
after janabah until the Day of Resurrection. 
On the other hand, urine is expelled as the excess from the drink consumed by 
humans, and feces is the excess from the food consumed.  
Thus, for those acts, wudu (ablution) is required.' 
The Jewish scholar said: 'You have spoken the truth, O’ Muhammad.'  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1 • Ilal al -Sharai’ Vol. 1, p. 282 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 537 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 179 • Tafsir Nur Al-Thaqalayn, Part 1, p. 599 • Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa’iq, Part 4, p. 55

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 17, H. 170
reference.chapter: THE REASON WHY GHUSL (RITUAL BATH) IS
reference.location: Volume 1 › THE REASON WHY GHUSL (RITUAL BATH) IS
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٧، ح ١٧٠

hadiths.chapter: 17. THE REASON WHY GHUSL (RITUAL BATH) IS OBLIGATORY FOR JANABAH (RITUAL IMPURITY) BUT NOT URIN
hadiths.en:
Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar -Ridha {a.s} wrote to Muhammad ibn Sinan in response to 
his questions: 
'The reason for the ghusl (ritual bath) of janabah (ritual impurity from sexual discharge) 
is for cleanliness, to purify a person from what has affected them and to cleanse their 
entire body, because janabah involves the whole body. Therefore, it is necess ary to 
purify the entire body. The reason for the lightening (of obligation) in the case of urine 
and feces is because they occur more frequently and continuously compared to 
janabah, so it is sufficient to perform wudu (ablution) due to their frequency, difficulty, 
and the fact that they occur without intentional desire or pleasure. Janabah occurs 
only through enjoyment or intentional acts involving themselves.'  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Ilal al-Sharai’ Vol. 1, p. 281 • Al-Manaqib, Vol. 4, p. 355 • 
Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 538 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 178 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 2

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 17, H. 171

reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١٧، ح ١٧١

hadiths.chapter: 18. THE CHAPTER OF RITUAL BATHS (GHUSL)
hadiths.en:
Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir {a.s} said:  
'There are seventeen occasions for ghusl (ritual bathing):  
On the night of the 17th of the month of Ramadan, the night of the 19th, the night of 
the 21st, and the night of the 23rd - on which Laylat al -Qadr (the Night of Decree) is 
hoped for.  
Also, ghusl on the two Eid’s (Eid al-Fitr and Eid al-Adha),  
Also, upon entering the two holy sanctuaries (Mecca and Medina),  
Also, on the day of putting on the ihram (for pilgrimage), the day of visiting (the sacred 
sites), the day of entering the House (the Kaaba), the day of Tarwiyah (the 8th of Dhu 
al-Hijjah), the day of Arafah (the 9th of Dhu al-Hijjah),  
Also, after washing a deceased person and shrouding them, or if you touch the 
deceased after their body has cooled.  
There is also ghusl for Jumu’ah, for a solar eclipse if the entire disc of the sun is 
obscured, and if you wake up and have not prayed, then you must perform ghusl and 
make up the prayer. Ghusl for janabah (ritual impurity from sexual discharge) is 
obligatory.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 382 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 304 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 18, H. 172

hadiths.chapter: 18. THE CHAPTER OF RITUAL BATHS (GHUSL)
hadiths.ar:
174 - وَّ رُّوِى .   
وق ال ن عض  مس اي ح ن ا: إ ن  إلعله  ق ى  ذ لك  إ ن ه ي ح زح  من  ذ ن ون ه ق ي ع ي سل من ها .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
'The ghusl (ritual bath) for janabah (ritual impurity due to sexual discharge) and 
menstruation is the same.'. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Amali (by Al-Saduq) Vol. 1, p. 647 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 
514 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 315 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 316 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 10, p. 400 • Bihar 
Al Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 23 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 18, H. 173

hadiths.chapter: 18. THE CHAPTER OF RITUAL BATHS (GHUSL)
hadiths.en:
It is narrated: 'Whoever kills a gecko (lizard) must perform ghusl (ritual 
bathing).'.  
[AL SADUQ] 
Some of our scholars have said that the reason for this is that by doing so, he is 
cleansed of his sins and thus performs ghusl. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 386 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 332 • 
Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 2, p. 515 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 18, H. 174

hadiths.chapter: 18. THE CHAPTER OF RITUAL BATHS (GHUSL)
hadiths.ar:
176   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ سَّمَّاعَّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ مِهْزَّإن َّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ْ ع ُّسْلِ إَّلْح ُّمُّعَّه ِ ق َّق َّال 
إ ِلأ َّ إ َّن  َّهُّ رُّج ِّضَّ لِلن ِّسَّاءِ ق ِى  إَّلس َّق َّزِ لِق ِل َّه ِ إَّلْمَّاءِ وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ إَّلْح َّن َّان َّه ِ وَّإج ِث   وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ إَّلْحَّي ْض ِ وَّ إج ِث   وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ إَّلْمُّسْي َّحَّاص َّه ِ 
وَّإج ِث   وَّ إ ِذ َّإ إِجْي َّس َّ ث ْ ن ِالْكُّزْسُّف ِ ق َّح َّار َّ إَّلذ َّمُّ إَّلْكُّزْسُّف َّ ق َّعَّلَّي ْهَّا إَّلْع ُّسْلُّ لِكُّلِّ صَّلاَّي َّي ْن ِ وَّ لِلْق َّح ْزِ ع ُّسْل  وَّ إ ِن ْ لَّمْ ي َّ ح ُّز ِ إَّلذ َّمُّ 
إَّلْكُّزْسُّف َّ ق َّعَّلَّي ْهَّا إَّلْوُّص ُّوءُّ لِكُّلِّ صَّلاَّة ٍ وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ إَّلن  ُّق َّسَّاءِ وَّإج ِث   وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ إَّلْمَّوْلُّوذِ وَّإج ِث     وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ إَّلْمَّي ِّث ِ وَّإج ِث   وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ  
مَّن ْ ع َّس َّلَّ مَّي ِّي اً وَّإج ِث   وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ إَّلْمُّحْزِمِ وَّإج ِث   وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ ي َّوْمِ عَّزَّق َّه َّ وَّإج ِث   وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ إَّلز ِّي َّارَّة ِ وَّإج ِث    إ ِلأ َّ مَّن ْ ن ِهِ عِل َّه   وَّ 
ع ُّسْلُّ ذُّج ُّولِ إَّلْن َّي ْث ِ وَّإج ِث   وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ ذُّج ُّولِ إَّلْحَّزَّ مِ وَّإج ِث   وَّ ي ُّسْي َّحَّث  ُّ إ َّن ْ لأَّ ي َّذْج ُّلَّهُّ إَّلز َّج ُّلُّ إ ِلأ َّ ن ِع ُّسْلٍ وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ إَّلْمُّن َّاهَّلَّه ِ 
وَّإج ِث   وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ إَّلأِسْي ِسْق َّاءِ وَّإج ِث   وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ إ َّو َّلِ لَّي ْلَّه ٍ مِن ْ س َّهْزِ رَّمَّص َّان َّ ي ُّسْي َّحَّث  ُّ وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ لَّي ْلَّه ِ إ ِ جْذَّى وَّ عِس ْزِي ن َّ سُّن  َّه   
وَّ ع ُّ سْلُّ لَّي ْلَّه ِ ي َّلاَّب ٍ وَّ عِس ْزِي ن َّ لأَّ ي َّي ْزُّكْهُّ ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ ي ُّزْج َّى ق ِى  إ ِجْذَّإهُّمَّا لَّي ْلَّه ُّ إَّلْق َّذْرِ وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ ي َّوْمِ إَّلْ ق ِطْزِ وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ ي َّوْمِ إَّلْأ َّص ْحَّى  
لأَّ إ ُّجِث  ُّ ي َّزْكَّهُّمَّا وَّ ع ُّسْلُّ إَّلأِسْي ِح َّارَّة ِ ي ُّسْي َّحَّث  ُّ .
hadiths.en:
It is narrated: 'Whoever deliberately goes to look at a crucified person must 
perform ghusl (ritual bathing) as a form of penance.'. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 386 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 3, p. 332 • 
Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 23 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 2, p. 515 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 18, H. 175

hadiths.chapter: 18. THE CHAPTER OF RITUAL BATHS (GHUSL)
hadiths.ar:
177  -  وَّق َّال رَّج ُّل  لِلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ ِن  َّ لِى  ج ِي زَّإن اً وَّ لَّهُّمْ ج َّوَّإرٍ ي َّي َّع َّن  َّي ْن َّ وَّ  ي َّص ْزِن ْن َّ ن ِالْعُّوذِ ق َّزُّن  َّمَّا ذَّج َّلْث ُّ إَّلْمَّح ْزَّح َّ 
ق َّا ُّطِي لُّ إَّلْح ُّلُّوسَّ إِسْي ِمَّاعاً مِن ِّى  لَّهُّن  َّ ق َّق َّالَّ لَّهُّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ل َّهِ مَّا هُّوَّ س َّى ْء  إ ي ِي هِ ن ِزِج ْلِى   
إ ِن  َّمَّا هُّوَّ سَّمَّاع  إ َّسْمَّعُّهُّ ن ِا ُّ ذ ُّن ِى  ق َّق َّالَّ لَّهُّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم ۝  إ ِن  َّ 
إَّلس َّمْغَّ وَّ إَّلْن َّصَّزَّ وَّ إَّلْق ُّو ٰإذَّ كُّل ُّ إ ُّولٰن ِك َّ كٰان َّ عَّن ْهُّ مَّسْو ُّلأً   ۝»إ َّسْمَّغْ ن ِهَّذ ِةِ إَّلْأ ي َّه ِ مِن ْ كِي َّاب ِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّز 
وَّ ج َّل َّ مِن ْ عَّزَّن ِى ٍّ وَّ لأَّ عَّح َّمِى ٍّ لأَّ ج َّزَّمَّ إ َّن ِّى  ق َّذْ ي َّزَّكْي ُّهَّا وَّ إ َّن َّا إ َّسْي َّع ْق ِزُّ إَّلل َّهَّ ي َّعَّالَّى ق َّق َّالَّ لَّ هُّ إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم 
ق َّاع ْي َّسِلْ وَّ صَّلِّ مَّا ن َّذَّإ لَّ ك َّ ق َّلَّق َّذْ كُّن ْث َّ مُّق ِي ماً عَّلَّى إ َّمْزٍ عَّط ِي مٍ مَّا كَّان َّ إ َّسْوَّإ َّ جَّالَّك َّ لَّوْ مِث  َّ عَّلَّى ذ َّلِك َّ إِسْي َّع ْق ِزِ إَّلل َّهَّ  
ي َّعَّالَّى وَّ إِسْا َّلْهُّ إَّلي  َّوْن َّه َّ مِن ْ كُّلِّ مَّا ي َّكْزَّةُّ ق َّا ِن  َّهُّ لأَّ ي َّكْزَّةُّ إ ِلأ َّ إَّلْق َّن ِي حَّ وَّ إَّلْق َّن ِي حَّ ذَّعْهُّ لِ أ َّهْلِهِ  ق َّا ِن  َّ لِكُّلٍّ إ َّهْلاً .   
وإلع سل كله سن ه  ما ج لا ع سل إلح ن ان ه  وق ذ ي ح ز ى  إلع سل من  إلح ن ان ه  عن  إلوص وء لأ ن هما ق زص ان  إج ي معا ق ا كن زهما ي ح ز ى  عن 
إ صع زهما .  ومن  إع ي سل لع ي ز ج ن ان ه  ق لي ن ذإ  ن الوص وء ي م ي ع ي سل، ولأ ي ح ز ي ه إلع سل عن  إلوص وء ، لأن  إلع سل سن ه  وإلوص وء 
ق زض  ولأ ي ح ز ى  إلسن ه  عن  إلق زض .
hadiths.en:
Sama’a ibn Mehran asked Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} about the 
Jumu'ah wash (ghusl). 
Imam {a.s} said: 'It is obligatory for both travel and residency, except that it has been 
permitted for women to forgo it during travel due to a shortage of water.  
The ghusl of janabah (ritual impurity due to sexual discharge) is obligatory, as is the 
ghusl of menstruation, and the ghusl for a woman experiencing istihadah (irregular 
bleeding). If She uses a cotton cloth to absorb the blood and the blood exceeds the 
cloth, She must perform ghusl for every two prayers, and for Fajr, She must perform 
ghusl as well. If the blood does not pass the cotton, She must perform wudu for each 
prayer. 
The ghusl for postnatal bleeding (nifas) is obligatory,  
The ghusl for a newborn (is obligatory),  
The ghusl for washing a deceased person (is obligatory),  
The ghusl for one who has washed a deceased person (is obligatory),  
The ghusl for entering the state of ihram (pilgrimage attire) is obligatory,  
The ghusl on the day of Arafah (is obligatory),  
The ghusl for visitation of Kaaba (is obligatory), except for one with a valid excuse.  
The ghusl for entering the Kaaba is obligatory,  
The ghusl for entering the sacred sanctuary (haram). It is recommended for one not to 
enter it without performing ghusl. 
The ghusl for the Mubahalah (mutual prayer for truth) is obligatory,  
The ghusl for seeking rain (istisqa) is obligatory.  
The ghusl for the 1st night of Ramadan is recommended, as is the ghusl for the night of 
the 21 st, which is a Sunnah, and the ghusl for the night of the 23 rd should not be 
neglected, as one of these nights is hoped to be Laylat al -Qadr.  
The ghusl for the day of Eid al-Fitr and the day of Eid al-Adha should not be omitted,  
The ghusl for seeking divine guidance (istikhara) is recommended."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 104 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 
3, p. 303 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 18, H. 176

hadiths.chapter: 18. THE CHAPTER OF RITUAL BATHS (GHUSL)
hadiths.en:
(From Mas'adah ibn Ziyad) A man said to Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq 
{a.s}:  
'I have neighbors who have female maidservant singers who sing and play the lute. 
Sometimes I go to the restroom and prolong my stay to listen to them.'  
Al-Sadiq {a.s} replied: 'Do not do that.' 
The man said: 'By Allah {SWT}, I do not approach them with my feet; it is only that I listen 
with my ears.'  
Al-Sadiq {a.s} responded: 'By Allah {SWT}, have you not heard Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, say: 
"Indeed, the hearing, the sight, and the heart - all of those will be questioned."' [Surah 
Al-Isra 17:36] 
The man said: 'It is as though I had never heard this verse from the Book of Allah {SWT}, 
the Exalted, from an Arab or a non -Arab. Certainly, I will abandon it and seek Allah’s 
{SWT} forgiveness.' 
Al-Sadiq {a.s} said to him, 'Arise, perform ghusl (ritual bathing), and pray as much as you 
like, for you were persisting in a grave matter. How terrible would your state have been 
had you died in that condition! Seek Allah’s {SWT} forgiveness and ask Him for 
repentance from everything He dislikes, for He dislikes only the repugnant things, and 
leave repugnant things to their people, for everything has its people.'  
[AL SADUQ] 
All ghusls (ritual baths) are Sunnah except for the ghusl of janabah (sexual impurity), 
and ghusl for janabah suffices in place of wudu (ablution), as both are obligations, and 
the greater one covers the lesser. If one performs ghusl for reasons other than  
janabah, they should begin with wudu and then perform ghusl, as ghusl does not 
replace wudu, since ghusl is Sunnah and wudu is an obligation, and a Sunnah does 
not replace an obligation.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Part 1, p. 116 • Tafsir Al-Burhan, vol. 
3, p. 535

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 18, H. 177

hadiths.chapter: 19. METHOD OF GHUSL FOR JANABAH (RITUAL BATH FOR
hadiths.en:
It is narrated: 'Eating while in a state of janabah (ritual impurity from sexual 
discharge) leads to poverty.'. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 418 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 2, p. 220 • 
Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 63, p. 384 • Bihar Al Anwar, Vol. 78, p. 49 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Part 1, p. 466 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 19, H. 178

hadiths.chapter: 19. METHOD OF GHUSL FOR JANABAH (RITUAL BATH FOR
hadiths.ar: 180 - وَّ ق ِى  جَّذِي ث ٍ إ ج َّزَّ ق َّال إ َّن ِّى  إ ُّرِي ذُّ إ َّن ْ إ َّعُّوذَّ .
hadiths.en:
Ubaydullah ibn Ali Al-Halabi said:  
‘Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} was asked whether it is appropriate for a man 
to sleep while in a state of janabah (ritual impurity from sexual discharge).’  
Imam {a.s} replied: 'It is disliked (makruh) to do so without first performing wudu 
(ablution).' 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 6, p. 423 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, vol. 1, p. 382 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 227 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 19, H. 179

hadiths.chapter: 19. METHOD OF GHUSL FOR JANABAH (RITUAL BATH FOR
hadiths.en:
In another narration, Imam {a.s} said:  
'I sleep in that state (of janabah) until morning because I intend to return (to intimacy).'. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 • Wasa’il Al-Shia, vol. 2, p. 227 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 19, H. 180
